priorities.label: S94 P1 — BUG: player colour stuck WHITE after rainbow/poop; must revert to one of the 6 PLAYER_COLORS
priorities.objective: Fix the bug where a player's colour gets stuck WHITE (0xffffff, NOT a player colour) after a rainbow shuffle or seagull-poop foul. It must always revert to one of the 6 PLAYER_COLORS. Root-cause: poop-foul render-tint revert (S89 P3) + rainbow '?? p.color' fallback preserving a stuck white.
priorities.scope: Investigate structureRenderer foul tint + seagulls/poop foul-expiry colour revert + rainbowLifecycle interaction; ensure the canonical colour is never set to white + always reverts to the seat PLAYER_COLOR; regression test. OUT: rainbow derangement semantics (works), NONET.
priorities.testing: tsc 0; vitest green + new regression test (foul -> expiry reverts to a PLAYER_COLOR; rainbow never yields white); preview spot-check.
priorities.check_method: S94 P1 (672cddd, pushed): white-stuck colour bug. ROOT: buildShuffleColorMap deranged over all 7 PLAYER_COLORS incl. bots-only Silver (0xc0c8d0) -> human could map INTO Silver; poop-foul reverts to it. FIX: SHUFFLE_PALETTE=PLAYER_COLORS.slice(0,6) (6 human only); Silver bot keeps Silver via ?? fallback; 7-seat uniqueness holds. +3 regression tests (no human->Silver over 500 seeds) + updated 2 palette-coupled tests (rainbow bijection, bots 7-seat). tsc 0, vitest 1466/1466 (2 clean runs). Pure-logic fix -> unit tests authoritative; user visual-confirms in playtest.

verification.needle: never has Silver as a key or value
6 humans shuffle, Silver bot stays Silver

priorities.label: S94 P2 — NONET tweaks: 9-of-any-single-type trigger (+ fire on sever) + timeout 120->180s + Codex supercombo entry
priorities.objective: (a) Generalize NONET trigger from 9-pure-Square to a connected component of exactly 9 of ANY single SparkType (9 circles, 9 spirals, ...) AND fire on sever/erase (not just placement) so 'build big then erase down to 9 same-type' works. (b) Timeout 120->180s (no solver -> no x2/no halve, already the behaviour). (c) Add NONET as a SUPERCOMBO entry in the Codex.
priorities.scope: sudokuEvent.detectNonet (any single type, was Square-only); placePrimitive trigger + severBond trigger hook; NONET_TIMEOUT_TICKS 7200->10800; codexStore/codexOverlay supercombo entry; tests. OUT: art/animation (P3).
priorities.testing: tsc 0; vitest green + detectNonet same-type tests (9 circles/spirals fire; mixed-type rejects; sever-down-to-9 fires; 8/10 reject) + timeout const test; codex entry renders.
priorities.check_method: S94 P2 (9333bbc, pushed): NONET tweaks. (a) detectNonet -> per-tick host SWEEP for a connected component of EXACTLY 9 of ONE SparkType (any type, not just Square), replacing the placement-only Square-only placePrimitive hook -> fires on build-up OR erase-down. Wired in main.ts host PLAYING tick, gated host-only + once-per-match (free after firing). (b) NONET_TIMEOUT_TICKS 7200->10800 (180s). (c) Codex: synthetic NONET_CODEX_ENTRY (id 'nonet' as GodlyId, name+hint, characterSprite='/art/nonet/kami.webp' Phase-2 path) appended to codexEntries; unlockGodly(NONET_CODEX_ID) on the trial rising edge. makeTile's missing-sprite catch renders name+hint until the Phase-2 kami sprite lands. GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1467/1467 (detectNonet tests rewritten for sweep + any-type/mixed/18-component); build OK; bundle 553.0 KiB (+3 over 550 charter, flagged). CHECK = build + full vitest (trigger exhaustively unit-tested) + user playtest. Codex tile not browser-verified (proven makeTile pattern + 404-sprite fallback). Resumed mid-edit after a context reset -> ran tsc first to map the half-edited surface.

verification.needle: export function detectNonet(world: World): PlayerId | null

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P0 #one-root-cause-can-explain-two-symptoms-trace-to-the-shared-state: 'stuck white after rainbow OR poop' had ONE root — buildShuffleColorMap deranged over all 7 PLAYER_COLORS incl. the bots-only Silver (near-white), so a human could map INTO Silver (and a poop-foul reverts to that same Silver). The render tints were all render-only + self-reverting, which RULED THEM OUT and pointed upstream at a STATE corruption (player.color) that BOTH triggers flow through. LESSON: when two different triggers yield the same wrong end-state, find the shared state they both pass through (one fix), not two; a 7th 'special' palette entry leaking into a 6-human shuffle is an off-by-one-set bug hiding behind PLAYER_COLORS.length.

P1 #a-per-tick-host-sweep-beats-a-per-event-hook-for-emergent-triggers: NONET must fire whether a 9-same-type structure is BUILT up or ERASED down. A per-placement hook catches only build-up (and would need a second hook on every erase/sever/bomb path). A single per-tick host sweep (detectNonet over all components, gated by the once-per-match guard so it's free after firing) catches ALL topology changes from ONE integration point at ~the cost of tickScoring's existing per-tick walk. LESSON: for a trigger reachable by many state mutations, sweep the resulting state once per tick (guarded) instead of hooking every mutation path.

P1 #a-synthetic-codex-entry-with-a-forward-compatible-asset-path: NONET isn't a godly recipe so it can't go through listRecipes()->entryFromRecipe. A synthetic CodexEntry (id cast to GodlyId, unlocked via unlockGodly on the rising edge) slots into the same grid. makeTile already .catch()es a missing sprite (tile stays image-less, name+hint show), so pointing characterSprite at the FUTURE Phase-2 path renders cleanly now AND auto-upgrades when the asset lands — zero rework. LESSON: when a UI slot wants an asset you don't have yet, point at the eventual path + lean on the existing missing-asset fallback for a forward-compatible stub.

SESSION #after-a-context-reset-mid-edit-run-the-failing-gate-first: a reset landed mid-P2 (sudokuEvent.ts fully changed incl. mintNonetSeed signature, but callers in placePrimitive/main.ts/tests NOT updated -> tsc broken). On resume I ran tsc FIRST to get the authoritative list of the half-done surface (2-arg call sites + renamed const) rather than reconstruct it from memory. LESSON: after a reset mid-implementation, run tsc/the failing gate immediately to diff what's actually half-done.
